diff --git a/docs/architecture.md b/docs/architecture.md

@@ -209,6 +209,11 @@ completed review during the active instance, and those targets form the lineariz
 one patch-agent session complete more than one reviewed patch without a later hash replacing earlier
 reviewed work in the linearizer prompt.
 
+After a patch agent's review completes, the controller appends a yes/no completion question to that
+patch agent's chat. A `yes` or `y` reply marks the feature done without sending a message to the
+agent backend. Any later message to that patch agent records the feature as reopened before the
+message is sent, so the same chat can continue producing patches and reviews.
+
 The command transcript is also the conversation history for the persistent `command-agent`. That
 system agent interprets chat sent to the Work Leaf command surface. It recognizes literal command
 lines and common natural-language requests for help, review, linearization, quitting, and launching
@@ -291,7 +296,10 @@ path.
 The terminal app maps a session to a left-pane `READY` marker when the controller exposes no loading
 state for that session. `TerminalUi` queues one terminal bell when a chat transitions into the ready
 state and renders ready rows in reverse video so they remain highlighted until the chat becomes busy
-again.
+again. A review-complete completion question focuses and highlights the patch agent chat so the user
+can answer yes or no immediately. A yes answer hides that patch-agent row from the left pane while
+leaving the session history available; typing in that selected chat records the feature as reopened
+and makes the row visible again.
 
 `src/ui.rs::TerminalUi` owns terminal-specific presentation state:

+9. If there are findings, the orchestrator sends those findings to the corresponding patch agent.
+10. The patch agent keeps patching through the orchestrator until the review agent reports no

 The current implementation path for this review loop builds patch-agent review targets through

 Reviewer `@work-leaf` directives are resolved in the reviewer conversation before output is
 interpreted as findings. If the reviewer finds issues, the orchestrator sends those findings to the
 patch agent. The patch agent then continues through the configured read path and patch protocol. When

 13. The review agent can request file text through the orchestrator before reporting findings.

item.text: The controller now gates the completion prompt on `findings_resolved`, and the new workspace test covers the `rounds=1 resolved=no` path. I’m doing final whitespace and documentation consistency checks.

item.aggregated_output:
204	Review bookkeeping has three scopes. The controller records a launch-time review baseline for each
   205	patch agent, tracks the latest reviewed hash for that patch agent so the same agent head is not
   206	reviewed twice, and asks reviewers to inspect every provisional commit from the active baseline
   207	through the latest patch-agent commit. `CommandChat` also keeps the ordered exact review targets that
   208	completed review during the active instance, and those targets form the linearizer handoff. This lets
   209	one patch-agent session complete more than one reviewed patch without a later hash replacing earlier
   210	reviewed work in the linearizer prompt.
   211	
   212	After a patch agent's review completes, the controller appends a yes/no completion question to that
   213	patch agent's chat. A `yes` or `y` reply marks the feature done without sending a message to the
   214	agent backend. Any later message to that patch agent records the feature as reopened before the
   215	message is sent, so the same chat can continue producing patches and reviews.
   216	
   296	The terminal app maps a session to a left-pane `READY` marker when the controller exposes no loading
   297	state for that session. `TerminalUi` queues one terminal bell when a chat transitions into the ready
   298	state and renders ready rows in reverse video so they remain highlighted until the chat becomes busy
   299	again. A review-complete completion question focuses and highlights the patch agent chat so the user
   300	can answer yes or no immediately. A yes answer hides that patch-agent row from the left pane while
   301	leaving the session history available; typing in that selected chat records the feature as reopened
   302	and makes the row visible again.
